mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling 3-month FEDFUNDS trend keeps discount-rate pressure low and supports equity duration; lower policy-rate momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if falling or flat 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by nonpositive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation crosses high levels while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, investors price future tightening and margin pressure; this inflation-policy gap predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above 5% and FEDFUNDS < 0.25%
regime_hint: inflation overshoot with delayed monetary tightening
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or the trigger has nonnegative average forward return.

mechanism_narrative: Large payroll gains combined with a falling unemployment rate signal broad labor-income growth and improving demand; the joint labor improvement predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change > 1000 and UNRATE 3-month change < -0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor trigger has average 60-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional average in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month INDPRO trend indicates recovering production capacity and fewer supply constraints, supporting earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes have a 20-day return hit rate below 50% and a Rank IC below 0.03 over a rolling 3-year test.

mechanism_narrative: Positive RSAFS momentum captures strong nominal consumption and near-term revenue growth; higher retail-sales momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the top tercile of RSAFS momentum fails to outperform the bottom tercile in 5-day S&P 500 returns across two consecutive walk-forward years.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals weakening residential investment and rate-sensitive demand, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% below its trailing 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of historical triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment combined with rapid CPI growth and a near-zero policy rate identifies an overheating labor-inflation regime; expected policy catch-up predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below 5%, CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation > 6%, and FEDFUNDS < 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if this overheating trigger produces higher mean 20-day S&P 500 returns than nontriggered observations with a one-sided p-value below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive quarterly increases in GDPC1 indicate expanding real activity and improving aggregate cash-flow expectations; positive real-output momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive two-quarter GDPC1 momentum has lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative momentum in any 5-year walk-forward evaluation window.
